api/v3/handlers/meters/query.purpose: Shared query-parameter translation sub-package for the v3 meter query endpoint. Converts api.MeterQueryRequest fields into streaming.QueryParams, validates dimensions and filters against meter definitions, and resolves customer IDs — insulating the HTTP handler from all domain-mapping logic.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue error factory
patterns.description: Every user-facing validation error is a package-level var of type models.ValidationIssue constructed with models.NewValidationIssue + WithFieldString/WithCriticalSeverity/commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute. Public constructors (e.g. NewInvalidWindowSizeError) attach runtime values via .WithAttr or .WithPathString.
patterns.example:
var ErrInvalidWindowSize = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeInvalidWindowSize, "...", models.WithFieldString("granularity"), models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))
func NewInvalidWindowSizeError(duration string) error { return ErrInvalidWindowSize.WithAttr("value", duration) }

patterns.name: Whitelist-only filter operator extraction
patterns.description: ExtractStringsFromQueryFilter and ExtractStringsFromQueryFilterMapItem explicitly reject every operator except eq and in by checking non-nil fields (Neq, Nin, Contains, Ncontains, And, Or, Exists) and returning NewUnsupportedFilterOperatorError. No permissive pass-through paths.
patterns.example: if f.Neq != nil || f.Nin != nil || f.Contains != nil { return nil, NewUnsupportedFilterOperatorError(fieldPath...) }

patterns.name: Reserved-dimension switch in BuildQueryParams
patterns.description: The dimensions loop in BuildQueryParams uses a switch on the key: case DimensionSubject, case DimensionCustomerID, default (meter GroupBy lookup). New reserved dimensions must be added as a case here AND to IsReservedDimension in dimensions.go.
patterns.example: switch k { case DimensionSubject: ... case DimensionCustomerID: ... default: if _, ok := m.GroupBy[k]; !ok { return params, NewInvalidDimensionFilterError(k) } }

patterns.name: Deduplication of GroupBy entries via slices.Contains
patterns.description: Every code path that appends to params.GroupBy (subject filter, customer filter, explicit GroupByDimensions) first calls slices.Contains to prevent duplicates.

patterns.name: CustomerResolverFunc dependency injection
patterns.description: Customer lookup is injected as CustomerResolverFunc (a function type), not as a direct service reference. BuildQueryParams receives it as a parameter so callers control the resolver — enabling noop injection in tests.
patterns.example: func BuildQueryParams(ctx context.Context, m meter.Meter, body api.MeterQueryRequest, resolveCustomers CustomerResolverFunc) (streaming.QueryParams, error)

patterns.name: ISO 8601 <-> WindowSize via lookup tables
patterns.description: Bidirectional conversion uses package-level maps (iso8601ToWindowSize, windowSizeToISO8601). Adding a new window size requires updating both maps and the error message string in ErrInvalidWindowSize.
patterns.example: var iso8601ToWindowSize = map[string]meter.WindowSize{"PT1M": meter.WindowSizeMinute, "PT1H": meter.WindowSizeHour, "P1D": meter.WindowSizeDay, "P1M": meter.WindowSizeMonth}

patterns.name: Filter complexity depth cap
patterns.description: Non-reserved GroupBy dimension filters are validated with f.ValidateWithComplexity(maxGroupByFilterComplexityDepth) (currently 2). Raising this constant widens the attack surface for nested filter expressions.
patterns.example:
const maxGroupByFilterComplexityDepth = 2
if err := f.ValidateWithComplexity(maxGroupByFilterComplexityDepth); err != nil { ... }

key_file_guides.role: Core translation function BuildQueryParams — the single entry point for converting MeterQueryRequest to streaming.QueryParams with full validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Map iteration order is non-deterministic in Go; GroupBy slice order may vary across calls — tests must not assert exact ordering.

key_file_guides.role: All typed validation errors for this package. Each error has a package-level sentinel var and a constructor that attaches runtime context via WithAttr/WithPathString.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HTTP status codes are embedded via commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute — never return raw fmt.Errorf for user-facing errors; use or extend these sentinels.

key_file_guides.role: Stateless conversion helpers for ISO 8601 durations, WindowSize, and QueryFilterString extraction. Only eq/in operators are supported.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ExtractStringsFromQueryFilterMapItem checks f.Exists (extra field vs QueryFilterString) — the two Extract functions are NOT interchangeable.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerResolverFunc type + NewCustomerResolver factory + CustomersToStreaming conversion. Bridges customer.Service to streaming.Customer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewCustomerResolver uses IncludeDeleted: true when listing customers; missing IDs are collected via errors.Join — both behaviours are intentional.

key_file_guides.role: Defines DimensionSubject and DimensionCustomerID constants, IsReservedDimension, and IsSupportedGroupByDimension. Single source of truth for reserved dimension names.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsSupportedGroupByDimension checks meter.GroupBy map — the meter must be passed with its GroupBy populated or all custom dimensions will be rejected.

anti_patterns: Returning raw fmt.Errorf for user-visible validation failures — always use a models.ValidationIssue constructor from errors.go.
Adding a new dimension key directly to BuildQueryParams without also updating IsReservedDimension in dimensions.go.
Appending to params.GroupBy without a slices.Contains deduplication guard.
Injecting customer.Service directly into BuildQueryParams — always pass it as a CustomerResolverFunc to keep the function testable without wiring.

Supporting additional filter operators (Neq, Nin, Contains) — the whitelist is intentional; extend only after updating ExtractStringsFromQueryFilter AND ExtractStringsFromQueryFilterMapItem consistently.

decisions.rationale: A function type is the minimal dependency surface; tests inject a one-liner lambda without needing a mock struct, and the HTTP handler passes NewCustomerResolver(svc) at wiring time.

decisions.decision: Strict whitelist for filter operators (eq/in only)
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse query complexity and index usage are bounded; rejecting Neq/Nin/Contains/And/Or at the translation layer prevents unbounded scan patterns from reaching the analytics store.

decisions.decision: Package-level ValidationIssue sentinels with runtime WithAttr/WithPathString constructors
decisions.rationale: Sentinel vars allow callers to errors.Is check the type; WithAttr attaches context without allocating a new type, keeping error handling consistent with the rest of the v3 handler stack.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new reserved dimension (e.g. 'workspace_id') that resolves like customer_id
